content.full_text:
PLEASE RESPOND TO
☐ SACRAMENTO OFFICE
STATE CAPITOL
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814
916-445-6637
FAX 916-445-0015

☐ 1326 H STREET
BAKERSFIELD, CA 93301
805-395-2927
FAX 805-861-9413

☐ 804 NORTH IRWIN
HANFORD, CA 93230
209-582-2549
FAX 209-582-3193

☐ 528 BARSTOW ROAD
BARSTOW, CA 92311
619-256-5805

☐ 1090 NORTH NORMA
RIDGECREST, CA 93555
619-446-4116

California State Senate

COMMITTEES:
VETERANS AFFAIRS
CHAIRMAN
AGRICULTURE AND WATER
RESOURCES
BUDGET AND FISCAL
REVIEW
INSURANCE, CLAIMS AND
CORPORATIONS

SENATOR

DON ROGERS

Sixteenth Senatorial District

August 3, 1992

NATURAL RESOURCES AND
WILDLIFE
RURAL CAUCUS


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 600
Sacramento, CA 95814

ATTN: Wayne Ortiz

Gentlemen:

The purpose of this letter is to request your guidance.
There have been several individuals and groups who have
come forward to help me financially in regard to
settling assessments against me by the Internal Revenue
Service.

The guidance I need from you is:

Can individuals or groups donate money to me, and if so,
in what form? Does this need to be into a special fund
and should their contributions be clearly labeled as a
gift? Is this reportable to you? Also, in the case of
personal loans to me, how should they be handled?

Your prompt response will be appreciated.

Yours truly,

DON ROGERS

DR/sc

embedding.qa_text: The letter is from Senator Don Rogers of the Sixteenth Senatorial District, dated August 3, 1992. It is addressed to the Fair Political Practices Commission at their Sacramento office. The senator is seeking guidance regarding financial contributions and loans received by him, particularly in relation to assessments by the Internal Revenue Service. Here is the full text of the letter: California State Senate SENATOR DON ROGERS Sixteenth Senatorial District August 3, 1992 Fair Political Practices Commission 428 J Street, Suite 600 Sacramento, CA 95814 ATTN: Wayne Ortiz Gentlemen: The purpose of this letter is to request your guidance. There have been several individuals and groups who have come forward to help me financially in regard to settling assessments against me by the Internal Revenue Service. The guidance I need from you is: Can individuals or groups donate money to me, and if so, in what form? Does this need to be into a special fund and should their contributions be clearly labeled as a gift? Is this reportable to you? Also, in the case of personal loans to me, how should they be handled? Your prompt response will be appreciated. Yours truly, [Signature] DON ROGERS DR/sc
